1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync/atomic"
6		"testing"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12	)
13	
14	// TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution is the regression test for
15	// ddx-0a651925 RC1: WorkerManager.Stop(id) must cancel the in-flight
16	// executor (and transitively the agent provider call) via ctx propagation,
17	// not merely close the poll loop. Pre-fix, runner.Run / RunAgent discarded
18	// the caller ctx by using context.WithCancel(context.Background()), so
19	// cancelling the worker goroutine left the agent subprocess / HTTP call
20	// running until SIGKILL. With ctx threaded into RunArgs.Context, Stop's
21	// cancellation propagates into the executor within seconds.
22	//
23	// The fake executor here blocks on ctx (exactly like a real provider's
24	// Chat method), so a successful test proves cancellation reaches the
25	// provider boundary.
26	func TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution(t *testing.T) {
27		root := t.TempDir()
28		setupBeadStoreWithReadyBead(t, root)
29	
30		var executorStarted atomic.Bool
31		var executorCtxErr atomic.Value // stores the ctx.Err() observed when the executor unblocks
32		executorDone := make(chan struct{})
33	
34		m := NewWorkerManager(root)
35		m.BeadWorkerFactory = func(s agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore) *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker {
36			return &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
37				Store: s,
38				Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
39					executorStarted.Store(true)
40					// Block on ctx — mirrors what a real agent provider HTTP call
41					// does when ctx is threaded all the way down.
42					select {
43					case <-ctx.Done():
44						executorCtxErr.Store(ctx.Err())
45					case <-time.After(60 * time.Second):
46						// If we get here, ctx cancellation never propagated.
47					}
48					close(executorDone)
49					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
50						BeadID: beadID,
51						Status: agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
52						Detail: "canceled",
53					}, nil
54				}),
55			}
56		}
57	
58		record, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
59			PollInterval: 30 * time.Second,
60		})
61		require.NoError(t, err)
62	
63		// Wait until the executor has actually started running so we know the
64		// cancellation we exercise is against an in-flight call.
65		require.Eventually(t, executorStarted.Load, 3*time.Second, 20*time.Millisecond,
66			"executor never entered the blocking Chat call")
67	
68		stopStart := time.Now()
69		require.NoError(t, m.Stop(record.ID))
70	
71		select {
72		case <-executorDone:
73		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
74			t.Fatalf("executor was not canceled within 2s of WorkerManager.Stop")
75		}
76		elapsed := time.Since(stopStart)
77		assert.Less(t, elapsed, 2*time.Second,
78			"Stop should cancel the running agent call within 2s; took %v", elapsed)
79	
80		observedErr, _ := executorCtxErr.Load().(error)
81		assert.ErrorIs(t, observedErr, context.Canceled,
82			"executor should observe context.Canceled from the caller ctx; got %v", observedErr)
83	
84		// Ensure the worker goroutine also exits.
85		_ = waitForWorkerExit(t, m, record.ID, 5*time.Second)
86	}
87

Check RoutePreflight introduction commit

data.output:
commit 978131c6598c65e05c77c4277bebc71313bafbaf
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 28 23:40:04 2026 -0400

    feat(agent): add routing-preflight gate before bead Claim [ddx-98e6e9ef]
    
    Add RoutePreflight hook to ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime that runs between
    nextCandidate and Store.Claim. When the hook returns the upstream
    typed-incompatibility error (or any routing failure), the loop exits with
    a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected (harness, model)
    pair — no bead is claimed, no executor invoked, no tier-attempt event burned.
    
    Defensive only: DDx consumes the typed-incompatibility surface from
    upstream agent.ResolveRoute and never duplicates the allow-list.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go            |  58 +++++-
 .../execute_bead_loop_routing_preflight_test.go    | 213 +++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 264 insertions(+), 7 deletions(-)

input.description: Find how RoutePreflight is used in agent package

data.output:
380	
381			// Routing preflight gate (FEAT-006 D3, ddx-98e6e9ef): consult the
382			// upstream typed-incompatibility surface BEFORE claiming. If the
383			// configured (harness, model) cannot serve the bead, exit the loop
384			// with a worker-level failure record — no claim, no executor
385			// invocation, no tier-attempt event burn.
386			if runtime.RoutePreflight != nil {
387				if rerr := runtime.RoutePreflight(ctx, harness, model); rerr != nil {
388					detail := fmt.Sprintf("routing preflight rejected (harness=%s model=%s): %s",
389						harness, model, rerr.Error())
390					if runtime.Log != nil {
391						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "routing preflight: %s (skipping %s, exiting loop)\n",
392							detail, candidate.ID)
393					}
394					emit("preflight.rejected", map[string]any{
395						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
396						"harness": harness,
397						"model":   model,
398						"reason":  rerr.Error(),
399					})
400					report := ExecuteBeadReport{
401						BeadID:  candidate.ID,
402						Status:  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
403						Detail:  detail,
404						Harness: harness,
405						Model:   model,
406					}
407					result.Attempts++
408					result.Failures++
409					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status

data.output:
commit c8a0515bf508c5b84116dc0440ca328098268077
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 29 12:59:44 2026 -0400

    feat(routing): make server worker escalation opt-in via spec.Escalate [ddx-c7081f89]
    
    Add WorkerSpec.Escalate field (default false) and change workers.go:650
    from implicit-on escalation to explicit opt-in, matching the CLI path's
    --escalate semantics. Plumb --escalate through executeLoopWithServer so
    the flag is forwarded to the worker spec. Add unit tests asserting
    ResolveProfileLadder call count per spec.Escalate value.
    Update docs/migrations/routing-config.md with server path alignment note.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/cli/internal/server/workers_test.go b/cli/internal/server/workers_test.go
index 87acfbfe..73adeee0 100644
--- a/cli/internal/server/workers_test.go
+++ b/cli/internal/server/workers_test.go
@@ -1105,3 +1105,57 @@ func TestConcurrentWorkersFromDifferentProjects(t *testing.T) {
 		t.Errorf("worker A artifact found under rootB — cross-project write detected")
 	}
 }
+
+// TestWorkerSpecEscalateControlsProfileLadder verifies that spec.Escalate=false
+// (the new default) prevents ResolveProfileLadder from being called, while
+// spec.Escalate=true preserves the tier-ladder behavior (ddx-c7081f89).
+func TestWorkerSpecEscalateControlsProfileLadder(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Run("disabled", func(t *testing.T) {
+		root := t.TempDir()
+		setupBeadStoreWithReadyBead(t, root)
+
+		agent.ResetRoutingCallCounters()
+
+		m := NewWorkerManager(root)
+		record, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
+			Once:     true,
+			Escalate: false,
+		})
+		require.NoError(t, err)
+		_ = waitForWorkerExit(t, m, record.ID, 15*time.Second)
+
+		if got := agent.ResolveProfileLadderCallCount(); got != 0 {
+			t.Errorf("ResolveProfileLadder must not be called when spec.Escalate=false, got %d", got)
+		}
+	})
+
+	t.Run("enabled", func(t *testing.T) {
+		root := t.TempDir()
+		setupBeadStoreWithReadyBead(t, root)
+
+		agent.ResetRoutingCallCounters()
+
+		m := NewWorkerManager(root)
+		record, err := m.StartExecuteLoop(ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{
+			Once:     true,
+			Escalate: true,
+		})
+		require.NoError(t, err)
+
+		// ResolveProfileLadder is called before any network I/O in the tier loop,
+		// so poll for the counter to advance rather than waiting for the worker to
+		// finish (which may block on agent connection attempts).
+		deadline := time.Now().Add(15 * time.Second)
+		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
+			if agent.ResolveProfileLadderCallCount() > 0 {
+				break
+			}
+			time.Sleep(20 * time.Millisecond)
+		}
+		_ = m.Stop(record.ID)
+
+		if got := agent.ResolveProfileLadderCallCount(); got == 0 {
+			t.Errorf("ResolveProfileLadder must be called when spec.Escalate=true, got 0")
+		}
+	})
+}

data.text: The issue is clear: `RoutePreflight` is always set, even in test-injection paths (`BeadWorkerFactory`), causing the preflight to fail with no service and preventing the executor from running. The fix is to skip `RoutePreflight` setup when `BeadWorkerFactory` is set.

input.command:
git add cli/internal/server/workers.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(server): skip RoutePreflight when BeadWorkerFactory is set [ddx-3f406266]

RoutePreflight was always constructed and passed to worker.Run, even when
BeadWorkerFactory is set (the test-injection path). In a temp directory with
no agent service, NewServiceFromWorkDir succeeds but ResolveRoute returns
no-viable-provider, causing the loop to exit before the fake executor runs.

TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution regressed when ddx-98e6e9ef
introduced the RoutePreflight gate: the gate fired before the executor,
so executorStarted.Store(true) was never reached.

Fix: only build routePreflight when m.BeadWorkerFactory == nil. The factory
path is a test-injection surface; real routing validation is not needed there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.output:
# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:

go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done

CGO_ENABLED=1 git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(server): skip RoutePreflight when BeadWorkerFactory is set [ddx-3f406266]

RoutePreflight was always constructed and passed to worker.Run, even when
BeadWorkerFactory is set (the test-injection path). In a temp directory with
no agent service, NewServiceFromWorkDir succeeds but ResolveRoute returns
no-viable-provider, causing the loop to exit before the fake executor runs.

TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution regressed when ddx-98e6e9ef
introduced the RoutePreflight gate: the gate fired before the executor,
so executorStarted.Store(true) was never reached.

Fix: only build routePreflight when m.BeadWorkerFactory == nil. The factory
path is a test-injection surface; real routing validation is not needed there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
All hooks passed and the commit succeeded. 

**AC summary:**

1. **`go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...`** — Server tests fully pass. Agent tests pass with `-short` (the pre-commit hook uses `-short -race`). `TestIntegration_OpencodeEcho` is a pre-existing environment failure (opencode binary installed but times out); the hook correctly skips it via `-short`.

2. **Default path correctness** — `escalationEnabled := spec.Escalate && spec.Harness == "" && spec.Model == ""` (line 655) ensures `escalate=false` (default) takes the single `singleTierAttempt` path — no `kind:tier-attempt` events emitted.

3. **`--escalate` preserves tier-loop** — `TestWorkerSpecEscalateControlsProfileLadder/enabled` (from `c8a0515b`) verifies `ResolveProfileLadder` is called when `spec.Escalate=true`.

4. **Bead-event taxonomy** — Default path calls `singleTierAttempt` directly; the `tier-attempt` and `escalation-summary` event writes are only in the escalation branch.

The fix in this pass: `RoutePreflight` is now conditional on `m.BeadWorkerFactory == nil`, restoring `TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution` which had regressed when `ddx-98e6e9ef` introduced the preflight gate.
